title: Bridge Essay: Decoding Reach Direction in Early "Visual" Cortex of Congenitally Blind Individu

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Imaginal Reconstruction** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Imaginal — mental imagery as real perception, imagination activating sensory cortices, aphantasia.

**Decoding Reach Direction in Early "Visual" Cortex of Congenitally Blind Individuals.**. J Neurosci.. PMID: 37783506. DOI: 10.1523/JNEUROSCI.0376-23.2023. Motor actions, such as reaching or grasping, can be decoded from fMRI activity of early visual cortex (EVC) in sighted humans. This effect can depend on vision or visual imagery, or alternatively, could be driven by mechanisms independent of visual experience. Here, we show that the actions of reaching in different directions can be reliably decoded from fMRI activity of EVC in congenitally blind humans (both sexes).
